section_i_academics_student_portion.honours_and_achievements:
Dean’s List, Faculty of Arts & Science (2019–2020, 2020–2021)
- Achieved a cumulative GPA in the top 10% of the Faculty, while managing a chronic inflammatory bowel disease (Crohn’s disease). This required careful time management around treatment schedules, frequent medical appointments, and occasional hospitalizations. For example, during Fall 2020, I completed final exams from the hospital while on IV steroids, maintaining A-range grades in Immunology and Organic Chemistry.

Undergraduate Research Opportunity Program (UROP) Scholar, Department of Immunology (2020–2021)
- Selected to join a research project investigating the role of gut microbiota in inflammatory bowel disease. Contributed to data collection, sample processing, and preliminary analysis under the supervision of Dr. Elaine Marshall. Co-authored a poster titled "Microbial Diversity Changes in Pediatric Crohn’s Disease Patients Under Biologic Therapy" presented at the U of T Undergraduate Research Symposium.

Crohn’s and Colitis Canada Student Volunteer Recognition (2020)
- Recognized for over 120 volunteer hours, including peer mentorship for newly diagnosed youth, facilitating online support groups during the COVID-19 pandemic, and assisting with virtual fundraising events.

Residence Academic Mentor (2020–2021)
- Selected as one of eight Academic Mentors in residence, supporting first-year students in adjusting to university academics. Organized weekly study halls and exam-prep workshops, with specific focus on inclusive practices for students with disabilities or chronic illnesses.

Scholarship in Secondary School (2019)
- Received the Ontario Scholar designation and the school’s Principal’s Award for top academic achievement in graduating class, with a 94% average in Grade 12 while undergoing treatment escalation for Crohn’s disease.
